title: Hail Damage Roof Insurance Claims in Lansing, MI | Commercial Roofing Contractors of Lansing
metaDescription: Documenting hail bruising and impact damage on Lansing membrane and metal commercial roofs to support an accurate, defensible insurance claim.

html:
Proving Hail Damage on a Membrane Roof Isn't as Obvious as a Dented Panel

Hail is a real recurring peril in mid-Michigan's spring and summer storm season, but it rarely produces the obvious dented-metal damage owners expect from hail claims in other parts of the country. On the single-ply membrane and modified bitumen roofs that cover most of Lansing's commercial buildings, hail impact shows up as bruising, granule displacement, and small punctures that are easy to miss from the ground and easy for an adjuster to wave off as ordinary wear if the documentation isn't specific. Standing seam and R-panel metal roofs, more common on newer warehouse and distribution buildings, show hail differently — as visible denting that is easier to point to but still needs measurement and pattern documentation to support a claim.

How Hail Actually Damages a Membrane Roof

On TPO, EPDM, and PVC membranes, hail impact bruises the material without always breaking the surface immediately, and that bruising can develop into a puncture or a weak point weeks or months later as the membrane continues to flex with temperature changes. On modified bitumen and built-up roofs common on older downtown and campus buildings, hail impact displaces the granule surfacing and can crack the underlying material in a pattern that follows the storm's hail swath rather than being evenly distributed across the roof.

How Hail Damages Metal Roof Systems

Standing seam and R-panel metal roofs, increasingly common on Lansing's newer warehouse and light-manufacturing buildings, show hail as visible denting on panels and sometimes on rooftop equipment housings. The denting itself is often more cosmetic than functional, but hail severe enough to dent standing seam metal can also compromise seams and fastener points that are not obvious without a closer inspection, which is why we document beyond just the visible dents.

Why Hail Claims Get Disputed More Than Other Storm Claims

Hail damage disputes are common because bruising and granule loss on a membrane roof can resemble ordinary aging if the documentation doesn't tie the pattern to a specific storm. We document hail damage against the actual storm track and impact pattern — clustering consistent with hail rather than scattered wear, comparison to unexposed areas of the same roof, and, where useful, a chalk test or core sample that shows the impact clearly. That pattern-based documentation is what separates a hail claim that holds up from one that gets challenged as pre-existing wear.

Timing a Hail Inspection Correctly

Hail damage on a membrane roof does not always announce itself with a leak right away, and waiting until a leak develops means documenting the damage well after the storm that caused it, when the connection between the two is harder to establish. We recommend a documentation visit within days of a significant hail event, even on roofs with no visible interior symptoms yet, because the physical evidence of hail impact is time-sensitive in a way a slow leak is not.

What a Hail Damage Assessment Covers

  • A full roof walk mapping impact locations and density across the roof, beyond isolated spot checks
  • Photographs and, where appropriate, chalk testing or core samples showing membrane bruising
  • Comparison against unexposed roof sections and rooftop equipment for storm-pattern consistency
  • Measurement and documentation of denting on metal roof systems and equipment housings
  • A written scope distinguishing hail-related damage from pre-existing granule loss or wear

What We Do — and What We Don't

We're your roofing contractor, not a public adjuster — we document and substantiate the roof damage so you and your adjuster work from an accurate scope. We do not characterize ordinary wear as hail damage, and we do not promise what a carrier will approve.

Hail Damage Claim Questions From Lansing Owners

How can you tell hail damage apart from normal roof aging?

Hail damage tends to cluster in a pattern consistent with a storm's impact area, while ordinary wear is more evenly distributed. Testing methods like chalk testing or core samples can also confirm impact bruising that isn't visible to the eye alone.

Does hail damage always show up right after the storm?

Not always, especially on membrane roofs where bruising can develop into a functional problem weeks or months later. That delay is exactly why documenting close to the storm event matters, even without a visible leak yet.

Is hail damage on a membrane roof covered the same as damage to a metal roof?

Coverage depends on the policy, not the roof type, but the documentation looks different for each. Membrane damage relies more on pattern analysis and testing; metal roof damage is often more visually apparent but still needs measurement and pattern documentation.

What if the adjuster says the damage is just wear and tear?

We can walk through the pattern-based evidence — impact clustering, comparison to unexposed areas, and testing results — that distinguishes hail damage from gradual wear, and provide that documentation for the owner to bring back to the conversation.

Should we file a claim for every hailstorm that hits the building?

Not necessarily. An inspection can determine whether a given storm caused damage significant enough to warrant a claim before that decision gets made, rather than filing reflexively after every event.
